page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.3xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 12 dedicated physical cores and 12 vCPUs, utilizing the Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 33 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. It includes 48.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks place the ecs.cm4.3xlarge in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is measured at 1575.58, while multi-core performance reaches 12474.34. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 698.22 GB/sec, which drops to 55.6 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. The instance demonstrates average capabilities in database operations with a PassMark database score of 3402.78 and Redis SET operations performing at 11.64 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in PassMark CPU physics and PassMark memory tests.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES cryptography, which assist in vector calculations and encryption tasks. Network connectivity is capped at a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Given the balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average performance across database, compression, and web serving benchmarks, this instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, mid-range database hosting, static web serving, and lightweight text processing or small-scale model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.3xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs and 12 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is configured with 48.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, but contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers average performance across standard benchmarks, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 12474.34 and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Supported instruction sets include AVX-512 and AES, which optimize cryptographic and vector processing tasks. With its balanced resource allocation and average database and web serving throughput, the ecs.cm4.3xlarge is best suited for general-purpose applications, medium-sized databases, and static web serving.

tagline: A dedicated twelve-core x86_64 virtual machine offering a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio and baseline network performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated physical cores and vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor at 3.2 GHz
48.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB RAM per core ratio
Baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps with zero bundled local storage
Support for AVX-512 and AES instruction sets for accelerated vector and cryptographic tasks
Well-suited for general-purpose computing, static web serving, and mid-range database workloads
